title: Container does not get automatically removed as specified by the  --rm flag after failing due to a  --publish port already in use
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

Container does not get automatically removed as specified by the  __--rm__ flag after failing due to a  __--publish__ port already in use. It works differently with Docker. After running the same command with Docker the container does not exist afterwards.

(I'm not quite sure, maybe this is less of a bug and more of a feature request?)


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

```
$ podman run -p 1200:80 --rm --name testing1 -d   docker.io/library/nginx
7772ea10f1f2c925073ddac648f2f68e8bdfc73aca81bd13f14a5fcb9a8b5c51
$ podman run -p 1200:80 --rm --name testing2 -d   docker.io/library/nginx
Error: rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:1200: bind: address already in use
$ podman container list -a | grep testing
7772ea10f1f2  docker.io/library/nginx:latest   nginx -g daemon o...  30 seconds ago  Up 31 seconds ago          0.0.0.0:1200->80/tcp  testing1
846ce730ed18  docker.io/library/nginx:latest   nginx -g daemon o...  18 seconds ago  Created                    0.0.0.0:1200->80/tcp  testing2
$ 
```
**Describe the results you received:**

The container _testing2_ exists (as shown by the command ` podman container list -a `)

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container _testing2_ shouldn't exist.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**


I did a test with Docker on macOS Monterey 12.5:

```
erik@N ~ % docker run -p 1200:80 --rm --name testing1 -d docker.io/library/nginx
856e65132b72c309fa2ddb31cf89eca8fc9d5d5f5a1317c6ba081625ffc5884f
erik@N ~ % docker run -p 1200:80 --rm --name testing2 -d docker.io/library/nginx
134b4f838b3fc31b114821aaed1257ac632cc123886cc5e2147ad475e9153778
docker: Error response from daemon: driver failed programming external connectivity on endpoint testing2 (624907e086df28f32f5809fbd183e2f1444a0bc4c1259e29744d8e436bbf374b): Bind for 0.0.0.0:1200 failed: port is already allocated.
erik@N ~ % docker container list -a | grep testing
856e65132b72   nginx     "/docker-entrypoint.…"   20 seconds ago       Up 19 seconds       0.0.0.0:1200->80/tcp   testing1
erik@N ~ % 
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Wed Jun 22 18:17:44 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body:
Thanks for the report, @eriksjolund!

It's definitely a bug.  Since @rhatdan is already working on it, I am assigning the issue to him.
